title: Consumer Behaviour
summary: Buyer decision process, factors influencing consumer behaviour, market segmentation, and targeting.
content_html:
Consumer Behaviour

The study of how individuals, groups, and organizations select, buy, use, and dispose of goods, services, ideas, or experiences to satisfy needs and wants.

The Buyer Decision Process (5 Stages)

  1. Problem/Need Recognition: Consumer recognizes a need or problem.
  2. Information Search: Internal (memory) and external (ads, word-of-mouth, reviews) search.
  3. Evaluation of Alternatives: Comparing brands/products on relevant attributes.
  4. Purchase Decision: Selecting and buying the product.
  5. Post-Purchase Behaviour: Satisfaction, loyalty, or cognitive dissonance.

Factors Influencing Consumer Behaviour

  • Cultural: Culture, subculture, social class.
  • Social: Reference groups (family, friends, social media influencers), opinion leaders.
  • Personal: Age, occupation, lifestyle, personality, economic situation.
  • Psychological: Motivation (Maslow's hierarchy), perception, learning, attitudes, beliefs.

Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs

    △ Self-actualization (creativity, purpose)
   △△ Esteem (status, achievement, respect)
  △△△ Social/Love (belonging, relationships)
 △△△△ Safety (security, stability)
△△△△△ Physiological (food, water, shelter)

Marketers appeal to needs at different levels.

Market Segmentation

Dividing a market into distinct groups of buyers with different needs, characteristics, or behaviours:

  • Demographic: Age, gender, income, education.
  • Geographic: Country, region, city.
  • Psychographic: Lifestyle, values, personality.
  • Behavioural: Usage rate, loyalty, occasion, benefits sought.

STP Framework

Segmentation → Targeting → Positioning

  • Segmentation: Identify segments.
  • Targeting: Select which segments to serve.
  • Positioning: Define how to be perceived relative to competitors (positioning statement).

Cognitive Dissonance

After a purchase, consumers may feel doubt ("Did I make the right choice?"). Marketers reduce this through reassuring post-purchase communications, strong warranties, and excellent customer service.

exercises.title: 5 stages first step
exercises.prompt: The first stage of the buyer decision process is:

exercises.title: Cognitive dissonance
options: Doubt or regret after a purchase
exercises.answer: Doubt or regret after a purchase

exercises.title: Maslow base level
exercises.prompt: At the base of Maslow's hierarchy are:

exercises.title: Reference groups
exercises.prompt: Reference groups in consumer behaviour include:

exercises.title: Positioning purpose
options: How the brand should be perceived relative to competitors
exercises.answer: How the brand should be perceived relative to competitors
